name: Qatar
summary: Day 76. **A3/A8 SECOND STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER — Day 4 of 7 since May 10 Safesea Neha bulk-carrier strike — NO 2ND STRIKE through May 14 morning** (CONFIRMED ABSENCE — UKMTO operational primary). The Doha bulk-carrier-strike story has now passed Day 4 of the 7-day window with **NO Qatar MoFA statement — Qatar institutionally absorbing the strike rather than escalating, consistent with the substantive-mediator posture**. Al Jazeera (Qatari state-aligned) carried Wang Yi-Iran framing favourably May 14, consistent with Qatar's mediator positioning and the Qatar-China alignment that today's BRICS-FM Delhi setting reinforces. **B4 TTF GAS €46.63 MAY 13 (basically flat, -0.11% d/d)** — at the €46 range yesterday's brief noted (€46.23 May 11); no fresh European Council action; Cheniere CCO Hormuz disruption framing carries forward; container freight FBX13 (China/East Asia → Mediterranean) at $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) carries forward. **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2 = TIME-SENSITIVE EXHAUSTION WINDOW MAY 16-23 (per markets dossier section E proposed trigger).** Qatar Ras Laffan production-halt remains the single most consequential supply-chain pivot in the cycle — 30% of global helium routed through it. PRIOR (May 13): **A3 SECOND COMMERCIAL VESSEL STRIKE INSIDE QATARI EEZ TRIGGER (May 10 + 7 days) — NOT TRIPPED through May 12 Day 2 of 7 sample-size 1, pattern undeclared. VESSEL ATTRIBUTION OF MAY 10 STRIKE CLARIFIED: Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed by NJ-based Safesea Group (logistics for UN peacekeeping, WFP, GSA) — strike on US-managed vessel during peace talks materially more politically loaded than May 10 unflagged framing. 2nd Safesea vessel hit cycle (Safesea Vishnu, March 12 STS Iraqi waters, killed one Indian crew). NO party has claimed.** **A12 SAUDI FM TRILATERAL MAY 11: Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan separately called Iranian FM Araghchi and Pakistani FM Ishaq Dar to discuss 'Pakistan's mediation efforts.'** Pakistan formally still official mediator; Qatar substantive (consistent with May 10 Axios scoop). Multi-channel architecture (Pakistan official + Qatar substantive + Saudi underwriter + Russia uranium-custodian standing offer) is publicly visible. **The trigger 'Iran responds via Qatar-IRGC channel rather than Pakistan-FM channel within 7 days' (added May 10) is at Day 2 of 7 — IRAN'S FORMAL MAY 10-11 RESPONSE WENT VIA PAKISTAN PER PUBLIC REPORTING (no Qatar-IRGC channel attribution); substantive-mediator-via-Qatar trigger has NOT been confirmed by operational signal yet.** Qatari and Saudi MoFAs remained publicly silent on the Doha bulk carrier strike (Day 2 of 7-day next-strike trigger). **Saudi visibly distancing from UAE's military track in light of WSJ Lavan scoop (B6).** HELIUM/LNG/FERTILIZER TRIPLE-CHOKEPOINT INTENSIFYING: Korean fab buffer ~4 weeks remaining as of May 12 (mid-June exhaustion). TTF €46.23 May 11 (+4.74% d/d, +5% from May 8 €44 baseline). Urea US Gulf FOB +50% since war began (Discovery Alert/Argus); UBS raised CF target $97→$140 explicitly noting Hormuz disruption. India DAP tender (IPL May 11) lowest offers $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast (vs $580-650 ex-crisis). Modi Hyderabad address May 10-11 (cut fertilizer 50%) reads as Indian-government public confirmation of Qatar Ras Laffan offline as binding shock, not hypothetical. The 'Qatar formally named primary mediator within 14 days' April 29 trigger is at Day 13 of 14 — multi-channel architecture has solidified into formal pattern; Qatar SUBSTANTIVE via IRGC senior generals but Pakistan retains OFFICIAL title. Qatari PM Mohammed bin Abdulrahman al-Thani met VP JD Vance in Washington Friday May 8, then changed planned return to Doha and instead flew to Miami Saturday May 9 to meet Sec. Rubio and special envoy Witkoff (CONFIRMED — Axios primary scoop by Barak Ravid; Times of Israel, Newsmax, Israel National News, TASS, Tribune India). Per Axios's source: 'While Pakistan has been the official mediator between the U.S. and Iran since the beginning of the war, the Qataris have been working behind the scenes... White House views them as especially effective in negotiations with Iran.' Critically: 'the Qataris are functioning as one of at least three back channels between the U.S. and Iran' and 'are using their contacts with senior Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps generals involved in Iran's decision-making.' First publicly-reported acknowledgment of: (a) at least three active back channels, (b) Qatar as the most-trusted Iran-side channel from WH perspective, (c) IRGC senior generals as Iran-side counterparties — NOT the FM channel. **Structural read:** Pakistan retains the OFFICIAL mediator title and parliamentary/spokesperson visibility, but the SUBSTANTIVE channel from the US side now appears to flow through Qatar to the IRGC. Political mediation (Pakistan) and operational mediation (Qatar→IRGC) now run on two parallel tracks possibly with different counterparties. **B1 BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA (~43KM) SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — MAJOR OPERATIONAL):** First commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM San Antonio (May 5) and FIRST near Qatar's coast — well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan, ordinarily considered the safest waters in the Persian Gulf. Small fire extinguished by crew, no casualties; UKMTO Warning notice posted; vessel flag/owner not publicly disclosed. NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY — IRGC has not claimed (consistent with May 5 CMA CGM 'claim-management discrimination pattern'). The geographic significance is acute: location is approximately equidistant from Iran's Bushehr coast and Qatar's main port complex. **Three reading frames operationally consistent:** (i) IRGC signal to Qatar that mediator role does not buy immunity for blockade-aligned shipping — concurrent with al-Thani Miami meeting; (ii) Houthi-style proxy operation (LOW probability — wrong geometry); (iii) misfire/accidental engagement (possible). No Saudi MoFA or Qatar MoFA statement at filing time. **HELIUM/LNG/FERTILIZER TRIPLE-CHOKEPOINT UNCHANGED:** Korean fab buffer 6-12 weeks per Smith/Discovery Alert = ~5 weeks remaining as of May 10 (mid-June exhaustion). Cheniere CCO 'astounded prices haven't risen higher with closure disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' TTF ~€44/MWh (-25%+ from March peak, +40% from pre-war ~€31). Urea Yuzhny FOB $616/mt May 6; DAP NCIC tender $880/mt fob (vs $840 Apr 4). The 'Qatar formally named primary mediator within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' April 29 trigger is at Day 11 of 14 — re-evaluation is now warranted: Qatar is NOT formally named in public-facing architecture (Pakistan retains OFFICIAL title) but IS the substantive Iran-side channel via IRGC generals. The trigger should be reframed to capture multi-channel structure rather than a single-mediator-named test.

Qatar MoFA institutional silence continues — multi-day pattern of substantive-mediator posture: absorbing the strike rather than escalating. Al Jazeera (Qatari state-aligned) carried Wang Yi-Iran framing favourably May 14, consistent with Qatar-China alignment that today's BRICS-FM Delhi setting reinforces. **B4 TTF GAS €46.63 MAY 13 (-0.11% d/d, flat)** at the €46 range yesterday noted; no fresh European Council action priced; Cheniere CCO Hormuz disruption framing carries forward; container freight FBX13 $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w). **Helium 2-week-clock at Day 73 since Ras Laffan halt March 2 = TIME-SENSITIVE EXHAUSTION WINDOW MAY 16-23** (per markets dossier E proposed trigger). 30% of global helium routed through Ras Laffan; SK hynix forced to diversify; Linde +1.9% today on industrial-gas allocation pricing.

recent_signals.headline: QATAR FORMALLY RE-EMERGED AS PARALLEL-AND-POSSIBLY-COMPETING MEDIATOR TO PAKISTAN VIA SUBSTANTIVE IRGC-SENIOR-GENERALS CHANNEL — RUBIO/WITKOFF/AL-THANI MIAMI MEETING SATURDAY MAY 9; AXIOS SCOOP REVEALS 'AT LEAST THREE BACK CHANNELS' AND IRGC SENIOR GENERALS AS IRAN-SIDE COUNTERPARTIES (NOT FM). Qatari PM al-Thani met VP Vance in Washington Friday May 8, then changed planned return to Doha and flew to Miami Saturday May 9 to meet Sec. Rubio + Witkoff. Per Axios's source explicitly: 'While Pakistan has been the official mediator since the beginning of the war, the Qataris have been working behind the scenes... White House views them as especially effective in negotiations with Iran.' 'Qataris functioning as one of at least three back channels' and 'using contacts with senior IRGC generals involved in Iran's decision-making.' Pakistan retains OFFICIAL title; Qatar is the SUBSTANTIVE Iran-side operational channel. Political vs operational mediation now run on parallel tracks. CONCURRENT: BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM May 5 and FIRST near Qatar's coast; small fire, no casualties; UKMTO Warning notice; NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY). Reads operationally as IRGC pressure-tactic on mediator coastline concurrent with al-Thani Miami meeting — Iran demonstrating that Qatar's mediation does not buy immunity for blockade-aligned shipping. No Qatar MoFA statement at filing time. No Saudi MoFA statement despite GCC fellow status. Triple-chokepoint (helium/LNG/fertilizer) unchanged — buffer clock 5 weeks remaining to mid-June.
sources: Times of Israel live blog May 9-10

recent_signals.verification: Qatar mediator-via-IRGC channel = CONFIRMED ACTION (physical meetings al-Thani Washington Friday + Miami Saturday; substantive negotiating activity REPORTED via Axios primary scoop multi-corroborated); Bulk carrier strike near Doha = CONFIRMED ACTION (vessel struck, fire occurred, UKMTO posted Warning notice); perpetrator UNKNOWN (no party has claimed); Iran neither confirms nor denies Qatar channel publicly; Qatar MoFA + Saudi MoFA = CONFIRMED ABSENCE of statement at filing time
